name: Harrison
address: Lot 9, Feilding — 'Arnott Street' (08.12.25 set) / 'Kiwitea Grove' (concept rev 4). Same house.
builder: Jennian Homes Manawatu (newer template than Beddis — project 25191, sheets A101–A302)
note: Verified answer key = the QS, always. The plans are the INPUT; these QS values are the expected OUTPUT. Primary baseline input is the concept rev 4 (6-page) set, whose design matches the QS (perimeter 60.4). The 08.12.25 set is an EARLIER revision (perimeter 59.89, porch 0.92) — run it only as a revision-robustness check, report don't grade. 'Correct' = matches this truth, not 'matches geometry' and not 'the three layers agree'.
what_harrison_adds: No separate window-schedule page — windows are floor-plan callouts W01–W14 (the no-schedule fallback path).
Different/newer Jennian drawing template — tests page classification + extraction generalisation beyond the one template.
Garage door reads 2150×4800 (vs Beddis 2210×4800) — tests the tolerant 2.0–2.4m height band still holds.
Multiple revisions of one job — core values should be stable across revisions.

garage_door.type: non-insulated in QS (plan legend says insulated)

openings_breakdown.note: QS 'WINDOWS MEASUREMENTS', by room, H×W. D73 = 14 opening rows = 11 windows + Garage Door 1 + Garage Door 2 + Entrance.

rows.note: narrow opening — NOT a sectional garage-door product; likely glazed garage opening

grading_decisions.answer_key: The QS, always. ground-truth.json = QS figures, never any single plan's printed numbers.
grading_decisions.primary_input: concept rev 4 (Lot_9_Kiwitea_CONCEPT_rev_4.pdf, 6-page) — grade against the QS key.
grading_decisions.secondary_input: 08.12.25 set — earlier revision; run as revision-robustness check only, report don't grade.
grading_decisions.windows: Do NOT pass/fail on a flat count. Primary check: does IQ extract the 14 W-code callouts off the floor plan (no-schedule path)? Cross-check robust QS figures: total opening area ≈ 46.89 m² and ext wall area 98.07 m². The 11-windows-proper figure is context, not a strict target.
grading_decisions.doors: Grade against QS (7/0/0) but treat plan-vs-QS disagreement as a KNOWN discrepancy. If IQ reads doubles off the plan (2/710, 2/610) that is IQ faithfully reading a plan the QS simplified — NOT automatically a bug. Do NOT tune IQ to suppress doubles to match the QS (overfitting). Report, flag, let the human reconcile.

grading_nuances: 'Window count' is ill-defined across jobs: plan = 14 W-codes; QS = 14 opening rows (11 windows + 2 garage doors + entrance); windows-proper = 11. These don't map 1:1. The QS cares about per-room opening AREAS (which feed ext wall area), not a flat count.

Plan ≠ QS on doors: plan hints at paired doors (2/710, 2/610, frame-to-frame 1430); QS records 7 standard / 0 double / 0 cavity. QS is the signed-off truth; IQ reading doubles off the plan could 'fail' against it — a clean example of why the human-review backstop matters.
open_question: Confirm what IQ feeds internal_wall_length_m into; if nothing in the QS consumes it, it is a diagnostic metric, not a deliverable (same as Beddis).
